# Hardware & the live stream {#sec-hardware}

This chapter explains how live EEG data reaches the decoder for Phase 2. You
only need it when running against real lab hardware.

::: callout-important
The live-stream path is **Windows-only** — it relies on
`tools/lslproxy/LSLProxy.exe`. Phase 1 and the test suite do not need any of
this.
:::

## The acquisition chain

Live data flows through several stages before the decoder sees it:

```
NeurOne amplifier → DigiOut (UDP) → LSLProxy.exe → LSL stream → decoder
```

- There are **two machines**: the *acquisition PC* (runs the NeurOne software
  and PsychoPy) and the *decoding laptop* (runs this application). They each
  connect to a separate Ethernet port on the NeurOne Main Unit — not directly
  to each other.
- NeurOne does not speak LSL natively. Its proprietary **DigiOut** output
  sends raw UDP packets.
- Bittium's **`LSLProxy.exe`** receives those UDP packets and republishes them
  as an LSL stream. The app launches and manages this proxy for you (see
  below); you do not normally run it by hand.

## Stream properties

The decoder consumes a single LSL inlet with these confirmed properties:

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Stream name | `NeuroneStream` |
| Type | `EEG` |
| Channels | 65 (64 EEG + 1 trigger) |
| Channel metadata | none — no channel names in the stream |
| Trigger channel | channel 65 (index 64) |

::: callout-warning
**Sampling rate must be confirmed in the lab.** It is configured in NeurOne as
1000 Hz, but a session has been observed reporting 5000 Hz. The sampling rate
materially affects preprocessing, so verify it before a real session.
:::

### Triggers are embedded in the EEG stream

There is no separate marker stream. PsychoPy's parallel-port triggers arrive
inside the EEG stream on the trigger channel. NeurOne packs several trigger
sources into one integer; the PsychoPy code is recovered with:

```
trigger_code = (int(raw_value) >> 8) & 0xFF
```

## How the app drives the proxy

`LSLReceiver` is a pure consumer — it resolves and pulls from a stream but
never publishes one. Publishing onto the network is the job of a
**`StreamSource`**:

- `LslProxySource` wraps `tools/lslproxy/LSLProxy.exe` (Windows-only) for real
  hardware.
- A replay source publishes recorded data for development off-hardware.

The app's session owns the active source: it is started when you discover
streams and reused by the run that follows (the proxy is **not** relaunched
between discovery and run), and stopped when the session ends.

## Units

::: callout-note
The proxy applies NeurOne's Tesla AC scaling before publishing, so LSL values
are already in **microvolts**. Whether the online preprocessing needs to
rescale these to SI volts has **not yet been verified in the lab**. Replay via
recorded BrainVision files delivers data already in SI volts, so no scaling is
applied for replay-based validation.
:::

## Before a real session — checklist

- [ ] Acquisition PC and decoding laptop are on the correct NeurOne Ethernet
      ports.
- [ ] NeurOne DigiOut is enabled and streaming.
- [ ] The configured sampling rate has been confirmed.
- [ ] `decoder_pipeline.joblib` from Phase 1 is available on the decoding
      laptop.

With the stream confirmed, proceed to @sec-phase2.
